title: lobelia decurrens subsp. decurrens
intro: Lobelia decurrens subsp. decurrens, also known as the decurved lobelia, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the family Campanulaceae and is a perennial herb with medicinal properties. Lobelia decurrens subsp. decurrens has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and aiding in detoxification.
